title: Walkera serrata
taxonomy: Walkera serrata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, which includes maples, horse chestnuts, and other related species. The plant is native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia, specifically in countries such as Malaysia and Indonesia. Its classification is further supported by its unique morphology and anatomical features, which distinguish it from other related species. As a member of the Sapindaceae family, Walkera serrata is closely related to other genera such as Cupania and Melia. Further research is necessary to fully understand the plant's classification and its relationships within the family.
uses: Walkera serrata, also known as wild garlic or rock garlic, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. This plant is valued for its health benefits, which include the potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and support digestive function.
